TextENGNew: The car pushed forward as if slicing through an abyss of darkness.
Not a single light in sight. Not a sound to be heard. Only my heartbeat pounded in my skull like some out-of-place noise.
The windshield began to fog over with moisture.
A thin mist hung over the entire village.
changeReason: Old says 'thick fog' but Japanese うっすらと霧 means 'thin mist' -- opposite description.

TextENGNew: The car's headlights cast their glow over the dull gray ground, but they felt somehow unreliable.
I had the feeling someone was watching me...
Silent and still, inching closer... looking down at me with gleaming eyes... Staring!
That couldn't be!!
Calm down! Get a hold of yourself!!
I just need to keep my eyes forward.
Forward...?
Even though I don't even know where I'm going...?
*Creak* *Creak* *Creeeeak* *CREEEEEEEEAK*!!!
With a shrill screech like fingernails raking across glass, the car lurched to a stop.
I unfastened my seatbelt and stepped out.
I peered into the space illuminated by the headlights.
*Drip*... *Drip*... *Drip*...
A stagnant surface of water... A bottomless swamp stretched out before me.
I let out a strangled cry and stumbled backward.
changeReason: Different action: 'immediately fled the area' vs 'stumbled backward'; Japanese 後退り means 'stepped back/retreated', not 'fled the area entirely'

TextENGNew: I spun around in a panic... and the car was gone.
Beyond where it had been, nothing but pitch darkness.
A tepid wind blew across the nape of my neck.
Groping my way forward, I came to a fork in the road.
Choose. Move forward. Think.
changeReason: Different verb: 'Make a decision' vs 'Think'; Japanese 考えろ means 'think', not 'make a decision'

TextENGNew: Choose! Move forward! Think! Choose! Move forward! Think! Choose! Move forward! Think!
changeReason: Same as entry 25: 'Decide' repeatedly changed to 'Think' throughout, matching Japanese 考えろ

TextENGNew: ``Maebara-san.``
A sudden voice jolted me awake.
``Are you all right?@k@vS18/10/161000040. You were thrashing around quite a bit in your sleep.``
changeReason: Different description: 'having a nightmare' vs 'thrashing around in your sleep'; Japanese うなされていました means 'moaning/thrashing in sleep', describing the physical symptom rather than diagnosing a nightmare

TextENGNew: Irie was in the driver's seat, peering at my face with concern.
It came back to me. I was in Irie's car, on the way home after dropping Shion off at her apartment in Okinomiya...
changeReason: New translation adds 'at her apartment' (自宅マンション), a detail present in the Japanese but omitted from the original

TextENGNew: At some point, sleep had overtaken me and I'd been dozing.
A nightmare, then...
Was I really awake? It didn't feel real. The raw, vivid sensations still clung to every part of my body.
changeReason: Old says 'body doesn't feel lively' but Japanese 生々しい感触が張り付いて describes vivid sensations clinging to body.

TextENGNew: I raised my reclined seat back up. My back was drenched in sweat.
Looking out the window, we had passed through town and were just reaching the outskirts of Hinamizawa.
``Sorry...@k@vS18/01/160100129. I'm just... a little tired, that's all...``
``That's understandable...@k@vS18/10/161000042. You went through quite an ordeal today...``
I recalled the words Shion had screamed.
It doesn't help anyone... If only Satoshi-kun were here...
Satoshi Houjou...
That name echoed in my mind like an incantation -- a curse.
changeReason: New translation adds 'incantation' from 呪文 (incantation/spell): 'like a curse' vs 'like an incantation -- a curse', capturing both words from 呪いの呪文

TextENGNew: Cursed or damned, I didn't care anymore.
changeReason: New translation includes both terms: 'cursed or damned' vs just 'cursed'; Japanese 呪われようが、祟られようが lists both cursing and haunting

TextENGNew: If it would save Satoko, I'd offer myself as a sacrifice without hesitation.
``Coach...@k@vS18/01/160100131. What kind of person was Satoshi?``
Irie furrowed his brow for an instant at the question. Then, after a sigh, he began to speak in a lowered voice.
``You've heard that Satoshi-kun ran away from home, haven't you?``
``...Yes, you told me before.@k@vS18/01/160100133. It's just that whenever Satoshi comes up, everyone dodges the subject...``
``I see.@k@vS18/10/161000045. For the people of this village, I suppose there are some thorny associations with that topic.``
changeReason: Different meaning: 'can't help but see the connection' vs 'there are some thorny associations'; Japanese 因縁めいた means 'fate-laden/karmic', and the new version better captures the nuance

TextENGNew: ``Did he really just... run away?``
``What do you mean by that?``
changeReason: Different question: 'What do you think?' vs 'What do you mean by that?'; Japanese それはどういう意味ですか clearly means 'What do you mean by that?'

TextENGNew: Irie's piercing sideways glance left me at a loss for words.
changeReason: New translation adds details from Japanese: 流し目 = 'sidelong glance' and 言葉に詰まってしまった = 'was at a loss for words', both absent from original

TextENGNew: ``No... I just have this feeling...``
``...Surely you don't mean... the curse of Oyashiro-sama...?``
``...I don't really know...``
A faint smile tugged at the corner of Irie's lips.
I couldn't understand why he would smile at that.
changeReason: Different meaning: 'Was he laughing? It was hard to tell' vs 'I couldn't understand why he would smile'; Japanese なぜ監督が笑ったのか俺には解らなかった means 'I couldn't understand WHY he laughed'

TextENGNew: ``...Do you believe in it, Coach?@k@vS18/01/160100138. The... the curse of Oyashiro-sama.``

``I'm in a profession that pledges itself to science, after all.@k@vS18/10/161000049. ...But since coming to this village, I've begun to feel a certain proximity to the supernatural, however slight.``
changeReason: Old says 'a man of science, it's part of my job' vs more faithful 'profession that pledges itself to science.'

TextENGNew: Irie broke off mid-sentence and switched on the wipers.
Milky-white semicircles traced themselves across the windshield.
Appearing and vanishing... appearing and vanishing... appearing and vanishing...
An endless, circular repetition.
The sound, marking intervals with the precision of a clock's second hand, grated on my nerves.
Yes -- it was the sound of a countdown. A reminder that time was running out.
It bore down on me, demanding a decision. Even if I ran, it would follow.
I felt as though I'd wandered into a maze with no exit, and an involuntary shudder ran through me.
``We've gotten off track...@k@vS18/10/161000052. If you want to know about Satoshi-kun, Shion-san might be the better person to ask.``
``I think she knew him far better than I did.``
Irie nodded with a solemn expression.
changeReason: Old says 'gave me a gentle look' but 神妙な面持ち means 'solemn expression' -- opposite emotion.

TextENGNew: ``Shion-san and Satoshi-kun were very close.@k@vS18/10/161000055. In a sense... yes, you might say they cherished one another dearly... a very special relationship.``
Shion and that guy Satoshi were...?
changeReason: New translation adds 'that guy' (やつ), the dismissive tone present in the Japanese but missing from original

TextENGNew: To Shion, Satoko was the only sister of the person she loved most.
So that was why she'd been fighting so desperately to help Satoko.
To think there was a connection like that between Satoko and Shion, when they'd seemed so unrelated...
It also explained the gap between Shion and Mion -- both members of the Sonozaki family, yet Mion watched Satoko's predicament from the sidelines with cold detachment.
changeReason: New translation adds 冷ややかに (coldly) and 成り行きを見ているだけ (just watching how things unfold), details from the Japanese missing in the original describing Mion's attitude

TextENGNew: ``If only Satoshi-kun were here...``
When Shion screamed those words, was her heart being crushed by her feelings for Satoshi?
Was I the only one... left on the outside...?
I couldn't shake off a nagging pang of jealousy.
Shion and Satoko were bound together by the existence of Satoshi.
There was no place for me in that circle.
But Satoshi was the one who abandoned Satoko and ran off on his own!
Just forget about a coward like that!
Satoshi isn't here anymore!
But I am. Me. I'm right here!
Shion's anguished cry was seared into my ears, refusing to fade.
If only Satoshi-kun were here...
He's already gone! Give it a rest, Shion!!
If only Satoshi-kun were here...
He abandoned both Satoko and you! Don't you get it? He threw you away!
If only Satoshi-kun were here... If only Satoshi-kun were here... If only Satoshi-kun were here... If only Satoshi-kun were here...
Those words repeated endlessly... like a cursed incantation.
changeReason: New translation adds 'cursed' from 呪い: 'like an incantation' vs 'like a cursed incantation'; Japanese 呪いの呪文 means 'cursed incantation'

TextENGNew: ``I'm home...``
I mumbled the words and headed straight upstairs.
``Welcome back.@k@vS18/00/162700002. Hey, Keiichi?@k@vS18/00/162700003. Dinner's ready.@k@vS18/00/162700004. Come downstairs, won't you?``
changeReason: Content differs significantly: original includes Mom's lines about looking unwell and encouragement ('Oh no, what's wrong? You look so down'); new version replaces these with shorter different lines. Japanese includes 辛気くさい顔しちゃって and いい顔に産んであげた

TextENGNew: Mom's carefree voice drifting up from behind me was grating right now.
changeReason: Old says 'harsh, thoughtless voice' but 能天気 means 'carefree/easygoing' -- opposite description.

TextENGNew: I just wanted to be alone.
Don't say anything more to me!
changeReason: Different meaning: 'I don't want to talk to anyone!' vs 'Don't say anything more to me!'; Japanese 俺に何も言わないでくれ means 'please don't say anything TO me'

TextENGNew: *SLAM!!*
I slammed my bedroom door shut, and without turning on the lights, collapsed face-first onto my futon.
The tension snapped like a thread. I felt the strength draining from every part of my body.
I didn't want to think about anything anymore.
I rolled over and pulled the blanket out from under me.
Suddenly, a sharp pain shot through my fingertips.
Is this... blood...?
Straining my eyes in the darkness, I saw dried blood caked thickly under my fingernails.
changeReason: Old adds 'getting all over the bed' not in Japanese; ベットリ means 'thickly/stuck,' not a bed.

TextENGNew: It must have happened when Teppei was beating me -- I'd dug my nails into the floor.
changeReason: Old says 'Teppei stomped on my fingernails' but Japanese means 'I dug my nails into the floor' -- wrong subject.

TextENGNew: I must have been gripping with tremendous force.
changeReason: Old says 'powerful attack' but Japanese describes narrator's own force, not an attack.

TextENGNew: When I closed my eyes, the image of Satoko's eyes as she rejected me floated vividly behind my eyelids.
It's so frustrating... But what can I even do? I gave up and ran away today, didn't I?!
Would my flailing only corner Satoko even further?
changeReason: Different verb: 'cause Satoko even more pain' vs 'corner Satoko even further'; Japanese 追い込む means 'corner/drive into a corner'

TextENGNew: ``Damn it!!``
*BANG!!*
I cursed and drove my clenched fist into the wall with all the force I could muster.
Everything felt futile.
I didn't care about anything anymore.
A bottomless sense of helplessness crashed over me.
Frustration gnawed at me. A sense of crisis bore down on me. I clung to the last shred of my self-respect.
changeReason: Different words: 'restlessness' vs 'frustration' (焦燥感 = frustration); 'sense of danger' vs 'sense of crisis' (危機感 = crisis); both word changes alter the psychological description

TextENGNew: And at the end of it all... despair.
A shadow lurked in the darkness.
I had the feeling someone was staring at me.
It was only my imagination, of course. But the way I felt right now, I couldn't laugh it off.
I pulled the blanket over my head, curled into a ball, and squeezed my eyes shut.
I wanted to forget everything and just fall asleep.
``W-who's there...?``
A strange sound echoed out of nowhere, and before I knew it I'd let out a voice so thin and frightened I could scarcely believe it was my own.
changeReason: Significantly different: original says narrator 'doubted his ears and faintly called out'; new says 'let out a voice so thin and frightened I could scarcely believe it was my own'; Japanese describes the quality of his own frightened voice

TextENGNew: Hee hee hee hee...
Grating laughter echoed around me.
``I said who's there!!``
I threw off the blanket and shot upright.
With a creak, the door opened.
changeReason: Old says 'I made my way to the door' but Japanese ドアが開かれた means 'the door was opened' by someone else.

TextENGNew: The bright light from the hallway flooded in, blinding me.
``Keiichi?@k@vS18/00/162700007. What's all that shouting about?``
When I squinted, I saw Mom standing there with a worried look on her face.
``...Oh, it's just you, Mom...``
All the tension drained from me at once. What was I getting so worked up about...?
``Oh, were you already asleep?@k@vS18/00/162700009. And it's not even dinnertime yet, honestly.@k@vS18/00/162700010. You need to eat something first.``
changeReason: Significantly different Mom dialogue: original includes lines about 'You look like you're not feeling well' while new version drops these and changes other lines. Different content conveyed

TextENGNew: ``Enough already.@k@vS18/01/160100148. I don't have any appetite, so just leave me alone.``
``Don't be silly.@k@vS18/00/162700013. That's exactly when you need to eat!@k@vS18/00/162700014. A person's strength comes from a full stomach, you know!``
changeReason: Different philosophy: original 'We're humans, we'll die if we don't eat' vs new 'A person's strength comes from a full stomach'; Japanese content differs from both but new is closer

TextENGNew: ``When you're feeling down, you need to eat twice as much as usual.@k@vS18/00/162700017. Three good meals a day, enjoyed to the fullest.@k@vS18/00/162700018. That's the secret to a healthy life.``
Rattling off her home-spun health tips, Mom flipped on the light and threw the window wide open.
Watching her bustle about from behind, a thought crossed my mind.
changeReason: Old adds 'she was a really hard-working mother' not in Japanese; JPN just says 'I thought.'

TextENGNew: Right... A family is about exactly this. Laughing together, talking about nothing in particular... It's built from the accumulation of all those unremarkable, everyday moments.
changeReason: Old adds 'no greater love' not in Japanese; new correctly renders 何でもない毎日の積み重ね (accumulation of unremarkable days).

TextENGNew: I had always believed without question that days like these would go on forever. Satoko probably thought the same... until three years ago.
Like tumbling into a pitfall... your ordinary life crumbling apart around you... That possibility existed for me too.
``I get it, I get it.@k@vS18/01/160100150. Yeah, sorry.@k@vS18/01/160100151. Good night, then.``
I burrowed back into the blanket to shut out Mom's fussing.
``What are you saying?@k@vS18/00/162700021. Get up.@k@vS18/00/162700022. Rena-chan is here to see you.``
Rena...?
When I went to the entryway, Rena was standing there, looking as though she didn't quite know what to do with herself.
changeReason: New translation adds detail: 'looking as though she didn't quite know what to do with herself' from 手持ち無沙汰そうな (fidgety/at loose ends), absent from original

TextENGNew: ``Rena... what are you doing here this late?``
changeReason: Old adds 'Did something happen?' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Yeah... well...``
``Did something happen?``
Instead of answering, Rena bit down on her lip.
Rena was staring fixedly at the blood on my fingertips...
Oh... I hadn't washed my hands, so the blood was still there.
Before I could explain, Rena murmured something under her breath.
``...Is his blood that red too, I wonder?``
A shudder ran through me.
For just an instant, I could have sworn Rena's eyes flashed red...
``Rena-chan, you really shouldn't have.@k@vS18/00/162700024. Bringing us this much -- it's too generous.``
Mom appeared in the entryway, arms full of large stacking boxes.
``I just made too much stew.@k@vS18/02/160200032. Please, eat as much as you like.@k@vS18/02/160200033. I hope you enjoy it.``
changeReason: Different food item and lost details: 'food' vs 'stew' (煮物 = simmered dishes); original includes 'lightly salted' and 'Hau~☆' which new drops

TextENGNew: In the blink of an eye, Rena transformed her expression and showed Mom her usual bright smile.
``Oh, of course.@k@vS18/00/162700026. Those croquettes you brought last time.@k@vS18/00/162700027. They were absolutely delicious.``
``Really?@k@vS18/02/160200035. I'm so glad... Hau~``
``My husband ate twenty of them in one sitting~``
changeReason: Old says 'My family' but うちの人 means 'my husband' -- wrong subject.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahaha, hearing that makes me so happy.``
There was Rena, chatting and smiling just as she always did.
But... was the Rena I'd seen just moments ago... merely my imagination...?
``Well then, Rena should be heading home now...``
As she wrapped up the conversation, Rena shot me a quick, sharp glance.
As if pressing an unspoken demand upon me...
``...Mom, I'll walk Rena home.``
``Good idea, it is late.@k@vS18/00/162700030. All right then, Rena-chan, see you next time.@k@vS18/00/162700031. Be careful on the way home.``
``Good night, ma'am.``
Rena stepped outside without waiting for me to finish tying my shoes.
After getting my shoes on, I turned to face Mom.
``What is it, Keiichi?``
``I'm not great at saying this kind of thing, but...@k@vS18/01/160100158. thanks for always making dinner and stuff.@k@vS18/01/160100159. I mean it.``
``Huh? What's gotten into you all of a sudden?@k@vS18/00/162700034. You sound like a bride on the eve of her wedding.@k@vS18/00/162700035. Is everything okay?``
``Forget it! Just forget I said anything!``
changeReason: Different expression: 'Shut up! Enough of that!' vs 'Forget it! Just forget I said anything!'; Japanese あぁもう！うるさい means 'Ugh! Shut up!'

TextENGNew: Overcome with embarrassment, I bolted out the front door without looking at Mom's face.
She was probably tilting her head in confusion behind me.
changeReason: New translation adds 'probably' (だろう = supposition) and 'behind me', correcting the original's presentation as stated fact

TextENGNew: But I just had to say it... I wanted to honestly express my gratitude for the peaceful, ordinary life I had here... and I was glad I'd managed to get the words out.
I walked behind Rena, who was striding ahead at a brisk pace, maintaining a strange distance between us.
changeReason: New translation adds 微妙な距離を空けて (maintaining a strange distance), a detail present in the Japanese but entirely absent from the original

TextENGNew: That distance, little by little but steadily, was growing.
changeReason: Old says 'distance gradually diminished' but Japanese 距離が開いていく means 'distance was increasing' -- opposite direction.

TextENGNew: Rena walked as though I didn't exist, staring single-mindedly ahead as she pushed deeper into the darkness.
A single line of light emerged from the gloom.
A streetlight illuminating a straight stretch of road.
Moths swarmed toward the center of the light.
*Sizzle* *sizzle*...
changeReason: Different sound: '*scrape*' vs '*Sizzle*'; Japanese ジリジリ is the sound of sizzling/burning, not scraping

TextENGNew: The rhythmic sound of moth wings burning against the fluorescent tube reached my ears.
We came to a fork in the road.
Without a moment's hesitation, Rena chose the path opposite the direction of her house.
changeReason: Old implies mistake ('wrong direction'); Japanese indicates deliberate choice of opposite path.

TextENGNew: ``...Hey, Rena, isn't your house the other way?@k@vS18/01/160100162. Hey, are you listening?``
Rena said nothing, only quickening her pace.
changeReason: Old says 'continued at her current pace' but Japanese 足を急がせる means 'quickening pace' -- opposite action.

TextENGNew: What was in that direction...?
I had no choice but to follow.
Our destination turned out to be the garbage dump at the dam construction site.
The scene -- illegally dumped waste piled high, its silhouette outlined against the darkness -- was eerie, heavy with an unsettling atmosphere.
If I remembered right, the body from the murder five years ago had been found here...
Even in broad daylight this place sent a chill down my spine, and now being here at night with just Rena... honestly, it was creepy...
Leaving me behind as I froze in my tracks, Rena plunged into the garbage heap without hesitation.
``Wait up, Rena!@k@vS18/01/160100164. It's dangerous in the dark!@k@vS18/01/160100165. You could hurt yourself!``
Either my words didn't reach her, or she was simply ignoring them -- Rena pressed onward, jaw clenched.
Rena scrambled over a massive industrial refrigerator and vanished beyond the heap.
``Heeey! Renaaa!``
My voice echoed hollowly through the silence.
Complete silence.
The surroundings were swallowed in an inky, pitch-black darkness.
``Answer me!@k@vS18/01/160100168. Renaaa!``
No response.
*Rustle*... *Rustle*...
Something stirred within the garbage heap.
A sudden chill prickled across my skin.
Growing anxious, I scrambled after Rena in a frenzy.
I tried to follow the path she'd taken, but the slope ahead was too steep -- there was no way I could climb it.
Where in that slender body of hers was Rena hiding that kind of brute strength?
Gasping for breath, I somehow managed to clamber to the top of the heap.
From the summit, I could see a small light.
It seemed to come from inside a station wagon buried in the scrap.
``Rena?@k@vS18/01/160100170. Are you in there?``
I approached the wagon and cautiously peered in through the sliding door window.
In stark contrast to its battered exterior, the interior of the wagon was neatly organized.
It was decorated with cushions in cute patterns that Rena would love, along with stuffed animals.
A faint sound caught my attention, and when I looked, I found a hamster in a small cage, watching me with its round, bead-like eyes.
This was... Rena's secret hideout...?
``Rena, you're in there, aren't you?@k@vS18/01/160100172. I'm coming in.``
I pulled the sliding door open and found Rena lying on her back on a mattress.
changeReason: Different position: 'sitting on a mattress' vs 'lying on her back on a mattress'; Japanese 仰向けになって means 'lying face up'

TextENGNew: Her eyes were wide open, staring up at the ceiling.
``Rena, you wanted to talk to me, didn't you?@k@vS18/01/160100174. Then... go ahead and tell me.@k@vS18/01/160100175. I'll listen.``
Rena slowly turned to face me.
``...This is Rena's hideout.@k@vS18/02/160200040. When things get hard, or sad, or lonely... this is where Rena comes...@k@vS18/02/160200041. To be alone...``
changeReason: Different emotion: 'angry, sad, or lonely' vs 'hard, sad, or lonely'; Japanese 辛い means 'hard/painful', not 'angry'

TextENGNew: ``When nobody else is around... nobody can do terrible things to you or say terrible things, right?@k@vS18/02/160200043. When you're alone, you can feel safe... you know?``
``...Don't say things like that.@k@vS18/01/160100177. You've got us, don't you?@k@vS18/01/160100178. We're your friends.``
``Ahahahahahahahahahahaha!!!!@k@vS18/02/160200043_2. Keiichi-kun, you sound like the protagonist of some TV drama~``

changeReason: Different criticism: 'How meaningless' vs 'How cheap'; Japanese 安っぽい means 'cheap/superficial', not 'meaningless'

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi-kun, you're always saying Satoko-chan is your friend, right? Right?@k@vS18/02/160200048. And yet... Satoko-chan is suffering all alone right now.@k@vS18/02/160200049. What good is a friend like that?``
``You come from a happy home, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200050. That's why you probably can't understand... the pain of losing everything...``
*Rip*... *rrip rip* *rip*......!!
Rena grabbed a fistful of her own hair, then tore it out with all her strength.
changeReason: Different intensity: 'tugged at it' vs 'tore it out'; Japanese 引き抜いた means 'pulled/tore out', stronger than just 'tugged'

TextENGNew: An entire clump of hair came away and fell to the floor.
``...It hurts so much...@k@vS18/02/160200052. Uuugh... it hurts...``
I had no words to counter Rena.
She was right on the mark.
Friendship... For Satoko as she was now, that word meant nothing.
I was ashamed of myself for brandishing the word 'friend' like some talisman all this time.
Pretty words and logical arguments weren't going to move reality.
I was furious at my own helplessness and cowardice.
But at the same time, there was a part of me curled up and crushed under fear and anxiety, defending itself.
changeReason: New translation adds 'defending itself' (擁護している), capturing the self-defense aspect present in the Japanese but missing from the original

TextENGNew: *Rustle*... *Rustle*... *Rustle!*
Garbage shifted and collapsed with a sound.
A mountain of refuse towering high -- a monument built from the mass of human greed.
The sound seemed like the scream of someone caught beneath it.
Her eyes wide open, Rena was crying.
The tears traced slowly down her cheeks and fell, one drop at a time.
I leaned in to look at Rena's vacant face.
Rena gazed silently into my eyes.
*Inhale*...
changeReason: Different sound: 'Haah' (exhale) vs '*Inhale*'; Japanese すぅ is the sound of inhaling, not exhaling

TextENGNew: As if to steady herself, Rena drew a deep breath.
``Keiichi-kun... Rena, you see...``
I nodded, keeping my eyes locked on hers.
``Rena, you see...@k@vS18/02/160200055. already... has to say goodbye to Keiichi-kun...``
What? Goodbye...?
``We'll never see each other again...@k@vS18/02/160200057. Rena is going to disappear from in front of Keiichi-kun...@k@vS18/02/160200058. So... farewell.``
``...Rena, what are you saying?@k@vS18/01/160100187. Farewell? What does that mean?@k@vS18/01/160100188. Why are you saying this so suddenly?``
``Rena is sad to say goodbye too.@k@vS18/02/160200061. But still... it's farewell...``
``Wait -- what happened?@k@vS18/01/160100190. Why won't you tell me anything?@k@vS18/01/160100191. Talk to me, Rena!``
changeReason: Different last line: 'Why are you keeping it to yourself?' vs 'Talk to me, Rena!'; Japanese どうして一人で抱え込むんだ means 'why are you bearing this alone', closer to original

TextENGNew: In my agitation, I grabbed Rena's shoulders with all my strength.
Rena's expression didn't change. She just stared up at the ceiling.
``Don't say farewell!@k@vS18/01/160100193. Don't you dare say that...``
Tears welled up, squeezing my throat shut.
I could barely breathe.
Unable to bear it any longer, I collapsed forward onto Rena.
Rena gently stroked my hair, as though soothing a baby.
And then, she whispered softly into my ear.
``...I'm going to kill Teppei.``
